rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for interface-level assumptions/guarantees and implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when generating AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should define interface behavior; implementation contracts should bind to subcomponents and connections.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees referencing ports must be in valid component type scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes or vice versa.
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE expressions unless model exposes typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
